executive_summary: The dataset contains 32561 rows and 15 columns, including 6 numeric columns and 9 categorical columns. The target is treated as classification for downstream workflow planning.
major_findings: Target is treated as classification with imbalance ratio 3.153.
primary_risks: High-cardinality categorical variables may require dedicated encoding strategy.
Class imbalance may distort naive accuracy and requires metric selection discipline.
recommended_next_steps: Review missingness pattern and define imputation strategy before feature engineering.
Review identifier-like fields and exclude leakage-prone columns before modelling.
Confirm target column semantics with business context before downstream training.
Prepare encoding plan for high-cardinality categorical columns.
Use stratified splitting and imbalance-aware metrics for downstream modelling.
